title: Best DVD set
wikitext:
[]: Best DVD set?             Quote Post by Castle » Mon Feb 14, 2011 6:33 pm I want to buy Evangelion on DVD, but I'm not sure which one to buy. I think I've narrowed it down to between the Holiday Pack and the Thinpack. I did some googling and found that a user here recommended the Thinpack in several posts because it supposedly has higher video bitrates due to the removed extras. Is this true? I would like the extras (mainly the clean OP/ED) but I would rather have better video quality if it came down to that.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Feb 14, 2011 6:38 pm Yes. It's true that the Thinpack has a higher bit-rate than the Holiday Special (which is really just the Perfect Platinum re-packaged anyway). Some of those extras are good, though. I mean, if you get past Matt Greenfield fanwanking his way through things and telling you how he recorded the English dub (who cares?), there are a lot of really cool storyboard/animatic versions of some of the later episodes, which is pretty neat to watch if you're into the some of the history behind the making of the Japanese animation for the series. But if you don't think the storyboards are cool just get the Thinpack, since the rest of the the Holiday's "special features" are only Tiffany Grant showing off her Asuka collection and what-not.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Feb 14, 2011 6:41 pm Related http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3439 also various other DVD related threads http://forum.evageeks.org/thread_lists/Everything_Else_Evangelion__threads.html

[]:             Quote Post by Castle » Mon Feb 14, 2011 7:11 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Yes. It's true that the Thinpack has a higher bit-rate than the Holiday Special (which is really just the Perfect Platinum re-packaged anyway). Some of those extras are good, though. I mean, if you get past Matt Greenfield fanwanking his way through things and telling you how he recorded the English dub (who cares?), there are a lot of really cool storyboard/animatic versions of some of the later episodes, which is pretty neat to watch if you're into the some of the history behind the making of the Japanese animation for the series. But if you don't think the storyboards are cool just get the Thinpack, since the rest of the the Holiday's "special features" are only Tiffany Grant showing off her Asuka collection and what-not.

[]: Re: Best DVD set?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Feb 14, 2011 7:44 pm Castle wrote: I did some googling and found that a user here recommended the Thinpack in several posts because it supposedly has higher video bitrates due to the removed extras. Is this true?

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Mon Feb 14, 2011 7:47 pm -

[]: Re: Best DVD set?             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Mon Feb 14, 2011 8:54 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: That would be me. You want the Thinpack, with SIX DVDs . The extras range from lousy to mediocre. You'll watch them once and then never again. Most of those extras are on the internet anyway. You can find that shit on this site, and on YouTube. I'll rip the clean OP and ED for you if you want. You want the THINPACK .

[]: Re: Best DVD set?             Quote Post by Castle » Mon Feb 14, 2011 8:56 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: That would be me. You want the Thinpack, with SIX DVDs . The extras range from lousy to mediocre. You'll watch them once and then never again. Most of those extras are on the internet anyway. You can find that shit on this site, and on YouTube. I'll rip the clean OP and ED for you if you want. You want the THINPACK .

[]: Re: Best DVD set?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Feb 14, 2011 9:08 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: That would be me. You want the Thinpack, with SIX DVDs . The extras range from lousy to mediocre. You'll watch them once and then never again. Most of those extras are on the internet anyway. You can find that shit on this site, and on YouTube. I'll rip the clean OP and ED for you if you want. You want the THINPACK .

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Mon Feb 14, 2011 9:14 pm I happen to have the Holiday Set and I don't see a problem. Also, are the thinpacks even available anymore? Last I checked, my local Best Buy only sold the Holiday Set.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Feb 14, 2011 9:21 pm I've only seen the Holiday set at Best Buy. I've seen individual volumes of the DVDs sold at F.Y.E. and the like. I mostly buy anime online anyway, since I can easily get the best prices for whatever set I want. Though I always check my local used media supply center before buying online. Sometimes I'll even find out-of-print material there.

[]:             Quote Post by Castle » Mon Feb 14, 2011 9:27 pm The thinpak is still in stock online at Walmart, Amazon, Rightstuf, Deepdiscount, and DVDPacific. Probably other places too.

[]: Re: Best DVD set?             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Feb 14, 2011 10:05 pm Castle wrote: I didn't even know there were director's cuts of 25 and 26...

[]: Re: Best DVD set?             Quote Post by Castle » Mon Feb 14, 2011 10:51 pm Reichu wrote: There aren't. The versions on the director's cut DVDs are just the versions released to video in Japan (first overseas DVD release has the Japanese TV version), but the differences are next to nonexistent.

[]: Re: Best DVD set?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Feb 15, 2011 8:27 am Clover wrote: Has anyone made quality comparisons for the thinpack and platinum? Is there any noticeable difference in quality like between Platinum and renewal?

[]: Re: Best DVD set?             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Feb 15, 2011 10:15 am Castle wrote: Last question for now I swear: what color are the subs on the DVDs?

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Wed Feb 16, 2011 12:00 am I own the Thinkpack and they're the best DVD versions to get. Excellent picture quality and sound (ever seen it on a 47-inch screen and 5.1 sound? DAMN. ...okay, it's not blu-ray, but I guess we have to wait a long while to see it in this format... yet again, what's the whole point of Rebuild?)... PLUS you have better episode structure. The version with extras are presented here: 1) 1 2 3 4 5 2) 6 7 8 9 10 3) 11 12 13 14 4) 15 16 17 5) 18 19 20 6) 21 (TV/DC) 22 (TV/DC) 23 (TV/DC) 7) 24 (TV/DC) 25 26 Now, Disc 6 is the worst, as not only 2 versions of 3 episodes were contained, there was audio commentary on one episode and a full animatic episode that ate up the disc...before questioning, no, I had not owned this version before, but did some research... Thinpack (the recommended package): 1) 1 2 3 4 5 2) 6 7 8 9 10 3) 11 12 13 14 15 4) 16 17 18 19 20 5) 21-24 (TV), 25 26 6) 21-24 (DC) (a perfect end that would lead up to End of Evangelion.) Notice that it uses more episodes per disc. It flows better this way. If you don't like the TV versions of 21-24 and rather not watch more than once for 25 and 26, skip watching the 5th disc and go to 6th disc.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Feb 16, 2011 9:25 am A while back I ripped both Platinum releases, and took the larger of each to get a composite of what would be the best quality for an AMV. As a result, here are the better, based on filesize/bitrate. 1-10, 18-26, 21'-24' from the Thinpack. However, the fullsize release does have some episodes at a higher bitrate: 11-17 from the fullsize The difference in quality isn't as great as the decrease in quality on some from thin to full. Disc six of the fullsize release is really rough on the episodes, trimming the filesize by a few hundred megs per episode. Overall, the Thinpack has the best quality. I find it very effective and elegant. Although, I do admit, I really like the extremely compact case that the holiday edition comes in.

[]:             Quote Post by JeffersonLeeEng » Wed Feb 16, 2011 11:14 am It may be a difference in authoring house for the full-size release. I will have to say some ADV releases (at least those that have been mastered by M.O.F.C, I think) do suffer from some really horrible video interlacing and even at times sound encoding problems. For one thing, I'm also not a big fan of downmixing certain Japanese soundtracks in very muddy 2.0-channel stereo. Granted, these simple mixes mostly come with the package of the original Japanese a/v setup, but I'm a little disappointed there isn't much quality assurance done when it comes here for a region 1 release. Still, I can live...for now...

[]:             Quote Post by ShinjiIkari » Sun Feb 20, 2011 9:13 pm I have Thinpack but I was only disappointed about the fact that it didn't come with a book. Only one piece of paper and not that great!
